body: Kept going, shipped WARRANT#93 and 93b, both compiled clean and synced to the terminal. Translated your brainscan/laser-triangulation metaphor onto what the codebase already has: RI_CoherenceIndex (indicator-pair agreement, already live) = laser triangulation; ComputeHTFAgreement (timeframe agreement, already live but only checks H1) = brainscan/multi-channel. WARRANT#93: found the same hard-cliff-vs-shrinkage bug WARRANT#85 fixed elsewhere, still present in RI_CoherenceIndex -- pairs with fewer than 30 samples were fully thrown away instead of down-weighted. Fixed with the same shrinkage math already used elsewhere (LE_ShrunkMean), wired into the live conviction trim. WARRANT#93b: added H4 as a genuine second, independent timeframe vantage point alongside the existing H1-only check -- real triangulation, not just a label. Both are bounded, reduce-only, fail-open trims -- same safety tier as the always-on mechanisms already running. Did NOT touch the P3B weight-search acceptance logic itself (the multiple-testing correction) -- that needs a full read of the multi-stage search first, flagged honestly as the real next step rather than rushed. Terminal wasn't running during this pass so nothing has been live-tested yet -- next attach covers WARRANT#91/92/93/93b all at once. Ledger at pass_75.
